stem: A decision-maker must choose among 3 crop selection options. The probability of favorable weather patterns is uncertain, estimated between 34% and 51%. The payoffs (in $K) for each option are: rice: $174K in good weather patterns, $45K in bad weather patterns. wheat: $184K in good weather patterns, $97K in bad weather patterns. soybeans: $230K in good weather patterns, $69K in bad weather patterns. Which option maximizes expected value? Does the optimal choice change across the range of probability estimates? What is the expected value of the best option?

ground_truth.answer: At P(good)=0.43: best option is soybeans with EV=$138.2K. The optimal choice switches: wheat at P(good)=0.34, soybeans at P(good)=0.51. EV range of best option: [126.6, 151.1].

solution_steps: Compute EV for each option: EV = P(good)×payoff_good + P(bad)×payoff_bad.
The optimal choice switches: wheat at P(good)=0.34, soybeans at P(good)=0.51.
common_failure_modes: Evaluating options only at the midpoint probability without sensitivity analysis
Not identifying the breakeven probability where the optimal choice switches
Ignoring downside risk and focusing only on expected value
